title: ranunculus aquatilis var. leontinensis
intro: Ranunculus aquatilis var. leontinensis, also known as sea buttercup or marsh crowfoot, is a variety of flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including rheumatism, arthritis, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be cautioned due to potential toxicity.
